logoAlt: A Long Journey to a New Land logo
title: A Long Journey to a New Land
intro: This exhibit invites visitors to explore the inspiring stories and immigrant cultures that shaped the heart and soul of Pueblo.
note: Use these cards to guide visitors into the exhibit panels, media, and supporting imagery.

cards.title: Exhibit Panels
cards.text: Step through the interpretive panels with images, tags, and audio narration.

cards.title: Exhibit Photo Gallery
cards.text: View photos connected directly to The Long Journey exhibit.

cards.title: Exhibit Media Library
cards.text: Watch and listen to audio and video pieces that deepen the story.

photoGallery.title: Images from The Long Journey
photoGallery.intro: This gallery supports the exhibit with visuals tied directly to the people, places, and stories featured in the panels.

items.alt: Historic building with people and vehicles
items.title: Arrival and Industry
items.description: A supporting image for the exhibit story.

items.title: Work in Motion
items.description: Use this slot for a photo connected to one of the exhibit panels.

items.title: Worker Portrait
items.description: An interpretive portrait or supporting archival image.

mediaLibrary.title: Watch and Listen
mediaLibrary.intro: These audio and video pieces expand the exhibit beyond the panels and images.

items.title: Exhibit Introduction
items.text: Add a short video to introduce the exhibit and orient visitors.

items.title: Oral History Clip
items.text: Use audio to add a personal voice to the exhibit experience.

panels.title: Origins
paragraphs: Industrial growth in Pueblo connected labor, transportation, materials, and ambition in ways that reshaped both the region and the people living in it.
This panel can introduce visitors to the broader forces that gave rise to the exhibit story and the communities connected to it.

panels.title: Workers and Families
paragraphs: The story of steel is also the story of neighborhoods, families, routines, sacrifice, and pride.
Use this panel to bring in oral histories, community memory, and human detail.

panels.title: Objects and Memory
paragraphs: Artifacts give visitors a tangible way to connect with industrial history.
A few well-presented objects can do more than a long block of text when paired with a strong image and narration.
